top_signals.title: US to hit Canada with 50% tariffs on wide range of goods
top_signals.summary: Washington accuses neighbour of engaging in unfair practices in move that could reignite trade war
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Stocks Extend Rally as Chips Rebound, Oil Declines: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: Asian stocks rose for the first time in four days as investors returned to chipmakers, driving a rebound in the sector after the recent selloff. Oil declined.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Grayscale files S-1 for first US Worldcoin ETF
top_signals.summary: The filing expands Grayscale’s growing lineup of crypto-related exchange-traded products outside of Bitcoin and Ether.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: China firm seeks damages over state control of British Steel
top_signals.summary: The UK government took over British Steel last year to prevent the closure of the country’s last primary steelmaking facility. China’s Jingye Group, which bought the firm in 2020, claims it is owed compensation. A Chinese steel giant demanded compensation from the United Kingdom government on Sunday, claiming the state’s takeover of its subsidiary, British ...

top_signals.title: Why India Could Dominate The Sustainable Aviation Fuel Market
top_signals.summary: Last year, the International Air Transport Association (IATA) revealed that sustainable aviation fuels (SAF) production represents only 0.6% of total jet fuel consumption, well below the 65% target by the global aviation industry to reach net-zero carbon emissions by 2050. SAF currently costs two to five times more than conventional jet fuels, with the hefty price premium creating a significant economic barrier for airlines that operate on razor-thin margins.  Additionally, there is not enough global refining capacity or raw materials such…
